architecture_summary: Add review-only runtime disablement rollback plan and trusted runtime clock receipt contracts, then submit both repo-local refs into runtime enablement review while preserving operator approval as the only missing input and keeping all runtime authority denied.
data_model_changes: Introduce a rollback plan with six non-executing rollback steps and required evidence refs.
Introduce a trusted runtime clock receipt with a bounded validity window and monotonicity requirement.
Update submitted evidence refs and review packet counts from nine submitted refs and three missing inputs to eleven submitted refs and one missing input.
implementation_plan: Add rollback plan schema, fixture, validator, and focused tests.
Add trusted runtime clock receipt schema, fixture, validator, and focused tests.
Register both schemas in the protocol manifest and CI worker runtime enablement lane.
Update submitted evidence refs and review packet builders, schemas, fixtures, and tests.
Update worker docs, schema README, gap register, and SDLC artifacts.
invariants: Rollback plan is not rollback execution.
Trusted clock receipt is not runtime authorization.
Dispatch, worker invocation, receipt emission, receipt append, and terminal closure remain denied.
Operator approval remains explicit as the remaining missing input.
rollback_plan: Remove rollback plan and trusted clock schema, fixtures, validators, tests, manifest entries, CI inclusion, docs updates, and SDLC artifacts.
Restore submitted evidence refs and review packet counts to nine repo-local refs and three missing inputs.
tradeoffs: The slice advances two evidence inputs without accepting evidence or authorizing runtime.
Operator approval remains deliberately external and unresolved.
